questions.text: In a transverse wave, the particles of the medium vibrate:
options: Parallel to the direction of wave propagation
Perpendicular to the direction of wave propagation
At an angle of 45 degrees to the propagation
questions.explanation: By definition, transverse waves involve oscillations perpendicular to the direction of energy transfer.

questions.text: Sound waves in air are always:
questions.explanation: Sound travels through air as a series of compressions and rarefactions, which are longitudinal oscillations.

questions.text: The distance between two consecutive crests or troughs is called:
questions.explanation: Wavelength (λ) is the spatial period of a periodic wave—the distance over which the wave's shape repeats.

questions.text: If the frequency of a wave is 50 Hz, its time period is:
questions.explanation: Time period T = 1/f = 1/50 = 0.02 seconds.

questions.text: The propagation constant or angular wave number (k) is defined as:
questions.explanation: The wave number k represents the number of radians per unit distance.

questions.text: In the wave equation y(x, t) = a sin(kx - ωt + φ), what does 'ω' represent?
questions.explanation: ω (omega) is the angular frequency, related to frequency by ω = 2πf.

questions.text: The speed of a transverse wave on a stretched string depends on:
options: Tension and length of the string
questions.explanation: The velocity is given by v = √(T/μ), where T is tension and μ is mass per unit length.

questions.text: According to Newton's formula, the speed of sound in a gas is proportional to:
questions.explanation: Newton assumed isothermal conditions, giving v = √(P/ρ).

questions.text: Laplace's correction to the speed of sound accounts for ______ behavior of gas.
questions.explanation: Laplace corrected Newton's formula by assuming that compressions and rarefactions happen so fast they are adiabatic.

questions.text: The speed of sound in a gas is independent of:
questions.explanation: At a constant temperature, if pressure increases, density increases proportionally, keeping the ratio P/ρ constant.

questions.text: With an increase in temperature, the speed of sound in air:
questions.explanation: Speed of sound v ∝ √T. As temperature rises, velocity increases.

questions.text: The principle of superposition states that the resultant displacement is the:
questions.explanation: The principle states that y = y1 + y2 + ... yn (vector sum).

questions.text: When a wave is reflected from a rigid boundary, the phase change is:
questions.explanation: Reflection at a fixed end causes a phase reversal of 180 degrees (π radians).

questions.text: In a stationary wave, the points of zero amplitude are called:
questions.explanation: Nodes are positions where the two interfering waves always cancel each other out.

questions.text: The distance between a node and an adjacent antinode is:
questions.explanation: Nodes are separated by λ/2; the point exactly in the middle of two nodes is an antinode (λ/4 from a node).

questions.text: A string of length L fixed at both ends vibrates in its fundamental mode. The wavelength is:
questions.explanation: In the first harmonic, L = λ/2, therefore λ = 2L.

questions.text: An open organ pipe produces:
questions.explanation: Open pipes support all integer multiples of the fundamental frequency.

questions.text: A closed organ pipe (closed at one end) produces:
questions.explanation: Closed pipes only produce the 1st, 3rd, 5th, etc., harmonics.

questions.text: The phenomenon of beats is a result of:
options: Interference of waves of slightly different frequencies
questions.explanation: Beats occur when two sound waves of nearly equal frequencies overlap.

questions.text: If two tuning forks of frequencies 256 Hz and 260 Hz are sounded together, the beat frequency is:
questions.explanation: Beat frequency = |f1 - f2| = |256 - 260| = 4 Hz.

questions.text: Stationary waves ______ energy.
questions.explanation: Unlike progressive waves, stationary waves store energy within the medium between nodes rather than transferring it.

questions.text: Which of the following properties of a wave is independent of the others?
questions.explanation: Amplitude depends on the energy of the source, whereas v, f, and λ are related by v = fλ.

questions.text: The speed of sound is maximum in:
questions.explanation: Solids have higher elasticity and bulk modulus, leading to higher sound speeds.

questions.text: A wave represented by y = A cos(kx + ωt) is travelling in:
questions.explanation: In (kx + ωt), the '+' sign indicates propagation towards the negative x-axis.

questions.text: The ratio of frequencies of the first three harmonics in a stretched string is:
questions.explanation: Strings produce all harmonics (f, 2f, 3f...).

questions.text: The ratio of frequencies of the first three harmonics in a closed organ pipe is:
questions.explanation: Closed pipes only produce odd harmonics.

questions.text: What is the phase difference between two points on a wave separated by a distance λ/2?
questions.explanation: Phase difference Δφ = (2π/λ) * Δx. If Δx = λ/2, Δφ = π.

questions.text: The frequency of a man's voice is 300 Hz and the wavelength is 1 m. The velocity of sound is:
Ultrasonic waves have frequencies:
questions.explanation: Ultrasound refers to sound frequencies higher than the human hearing limit.

questions.text: The velocity of sound in vacuum is:
questions.explanation: Sound is a mechanical wave and requires a material medium to propagate.

questions.text: In a longitudinal wave, a compression is a region of:
questions.explanation: Particles are crowded together in a compression, increasing both pressure and density.

questions.text: The speed of sound in air at STP is approximately:
questions.explanation: Standard value used in Physics at 0°C (STP).

questions.text: A pipe open at both ends has a fundamental frequency 'f'. If one end is closed, the new fundamental frequency will be:
questions.explanation: f_open = v/2L and f_closed = v/4L. Thus f_closed = f_open / 2.

questions.text: Two waves y1 = A sin(ωt) and y2 = A cos(ωt) have a phase difference of:
questions.explanation: cos(ωt) can be written as sin(ωt + π/2).

questions.text: Which of the following is not a characteristic of a musical sound?
questions.explanation: Pitch, Quality, and Loudness are the three psychological characteristics of musical sound.

questions.text: Pitch of a sound depends on its:
questions.explanation: Higher frequency corresponds to a higher pitch.

questions.text: Quality of sound depends on:
questions.explanation: Quality allows us to distinguish between two sounds of same pitch and loudness based on their harmonic content.

questions.text: The intensity of a wave is proportional to the ______ of its amplitude.
questions.explanation: Intensity I ∝ A².

questions.text: Beats can be heard only if the frequency difference is less than about:
questions.explanation: Due to persistence of hearing, the human ear can distinguish beats only up to about 7-10 per second.

questions.text: The speed of sound increases with increase in ______ of the air.
questions.explanation: Moist air is less dense than dry air, and since v ∝ 1/√ρ, speed increases with humidity.
